meaning: v. 提倡，拥护；n. 提倡者，拥护者
example: We advocate the use of renewable energy.

meaning: adj. 模棱两可的，含糊不清的
example: The instructions were too ambiguous to follow.

meaning: v. 把...归因于；n. 属性，特征
example: He attributes his success to hard work.

meaning: adj. 真正的，真实的，可信的
example: This is an authentic Italian restaurant.

meaning: n. 偏见，偏向；v. 使有偏见
example: We must avoid bias in our research.
The stadium has a seating capacity of 50,000.
Under no circumstances should you open this door.

meaning: v. 同时发生，相符，一致
example: My birthday coincides with a national holiday.
Nothing can compensate for the loss of a loved one.
We need a comprehensive plan to tackle the issue.
We can conclude that the experiment was successful.

meaning: v. 限制，禁闭；n. 界限
example: Please confine your remarks to the topic at hand.
There is a growing consensus on this issue.
You must accept the consequences of your actions.
Twelve months constitute a year.
The evidence contradicts his statement.
The new policy caused much controversy.
The committee will convene next week.

meaning: v. 协调，配合；n. 坐标
example: We need to coordinate our efforts.
Her account of the event corresponds with yours.
What are the criteria for selection?

meaning: adj. 至关重要的，决定性的
example: Early diagnosis is crucial for effective treatment.
We can deduce from the text that the author is optimistic.
The study demonstrates the link between smoking and cancer.
They were deprived of their basic rights.
Many English words are derived from Latin.
The weather deteriorated rapidly.
She faces a moral dilemma.

meaning: adj. 明显的，独特的，不同的
example: There is a distinct improvement in your work.
Don't distort the truth.
The team dominated the game from start to finish.
We need to eliminate waste.
New problems emerged during the discussion.

meaning: v. 遭遇，遇到；n. 遭遇
example: We encountered some difficulties.
Use technology to enhance learning.
Please ensure that the door is locked.

meaning: v. 估计，估价；n. 估计
example: They estimated the cost at $1000.
We need to evaluate the effectiveness of the program.
It was evident that she was unhappy.
The company has evolved over the years.
We cannot exclude the possibility of error.
She gave explicit instructions.
The new improved layout will facilitate work.
Prices fluctuate according to supply and demand.
Freedom of speech is a fundamental right.
The project generated a lot of interest.

meaning: v. 保证，担保；n. 保证书
example: We guarantee satisfaction.
Wait for evidence to support the hypothesis.
She identified the man as her attacker.
He is ignorant of the facts.

meaning: v. 说明，阐明，给...作插图
example: Let me illustrate this point with a story.
His silence implied consent.
Don't impose your views on others.
Money is a strong incentive.
The new car incorporates many safety features.

meaning: adj. 不可避免的，必然的
example: Change is inevitable.
She inherited a fortune from her father.
The government initiated a new program.
Companies must innovate to survive.
We need to integrate theory with practice.
Don't interfere in my business.
How do you interpret this poem?
The police had to intervene.
Police are investigating the cause of the fire.
The end justifies the means.
The company launched a new product.
He has a legitimate claim to the property.
We must maintain law and order.

meaning: v. 显示，表明；adj. 明显的
example: His anxiety was manifest to everyone.
She knows how to manipulate people.
We need to modify the plan.
Respect should be mutual.
The two sides are negotiating a peace treaty.
Where can I obtain a copy of the report?
Work occupies most of her time.
He perceived a change in her attitude.
If symptoms persist, consult a doctor.
Try to see things from my perspective.
The project is in its final phase.
Globalization is a complex phenomenon.
He has the potential to be a great leader.
Please be precise.
It is hard to predict the future.
The preliminary results are encouraging.
I presume you are tired after the journey.
Safety is our top priority.
Smoking is prohibited in this building.
Exercise promotes health.
A large proportion of the budget is spent on defense.
There is little prospect of success.
She decided to pursue a career in medicine.
We need radical changes.
The winner will be chosen at random.
Reinforce good behavior with rewards.
The proposal was rejected.
Please provide all relevant information.
You can rely on me.
Speed is restricted to 30 mph.
The investigation revealed the truth.
I need to revise for the exam.
Put the cards in alphabetical sequence.
The computer simulates the flight conditions.
Be more specific.
The patient's condition is stable.
We need a new marketing strategy.
The structure of the organization is complex.
Subsequent events proved him wrong.
You can substitute honey for sugar.
We have sufficient food for everyone.
Hope sustained him during his illness.
Our target is to increase sales by 10%.
The report is full of technical terms.
We need to improve our teaching techniques.
This is just a temporary solution.
There is a tendency towards smaller families.
The internet has transformed our lives.
The disease is transmitted by mosquitoes.
Our ultimate goal is peace.
Everyone's fingerprints are unique.
Do you have a valid passport?
Opinions vary on this subject.
The stars are visible tonight.
She volunteered to help.

meaning: v. 废除，废止（制度/法律）
example: The government abolished the death penalty.
He accumulated a great deal of knowledge.
They live in an affluent neighborhood.

meaning: n. 指控，断言（未经证实）
example: He denied the allegation of fraud.

meaning: v. 减轻，缓和（痛苦/问题）
example: This medicine will alleviate the pain.

meaning: adj. 矛盾的，好坏参半的
example: I feel ambivalent about moving abroad.

meaning: v. 清晰表达；adj. 能言善辩的
example: She articulated her concerns clearly.

meaning: v. 渴望，追求（崇高目标）
example: She aspires to become a doctor.
His assertion that prices will fall seems optimistic.
The region has been granted autonomous status.
The new law provoked a public backlash.

meaning: adj. 良性的，温和的，有益的
example: The tumor turned out to be benign.

meaning: n. 大灾难，灾难性的失败
example: The flood was an environmental catastrophe.

meaning: adj. 连贯的，有条理的，清晰的
example: Please make your argument more coherent.
The wine complements the food perfectly.

meaning: adj. 自满的，得意的（含贬义）
example: We cannot afford to be complacent about safety.

meaning: v. 承认（失败/错误），让步
example: He conceded that he had made a mistake.

meaning: adj. 同时发生的，并发的
example: The two events were concurrent.

meaning: v. 宽恕，纵容（不道德行为）
example: We cannot condone such behavior.

meaning: v. 使困惑，使混淆；打乱（预期）
example: The results confounded expectations.
She contemplated her future with uncertainty.
His contention is that taxes should be lower.
Abortion is a contentious issue.
She spoke with great conviction.

meaning: v. 使相互关联；n. 相关物
example: Wealth does not always correlate with happiness.

meaning: n. 对应物，职位相当的人
example: The minister met with his French counterpart.

meaning: n. 资质，证明文件，资历
example: She has impressive academic credentials.

meaning: v. 认为，视为（正式用语）
example: The proposal was deemed inappropriate.
Vitamin D deficiency can cause bone problems.

meaning: adj. 故意的；v. 仔细考虑
example: The delay was deliberate.

meaning: adj. 人口统计的；n. 人口特征
example: The demographic profile of the city has changed.
The film depicts life in wartime China.

meaning: n. 差异，不一致，矛盾之处
example: There is a discrepancy between the two reports.
She has a cheerful disposition.

meaning: adj. 占主导地位的，最有影响力的
example: English is the dominant language in business.

meaning: adj. 教会的，教会制度的
example: The building has great ecclesiastical significance.

meaning: adj. 经验性的，以实验为依据的
example: The study is based on empirical evidence.
The committee endorsed the proposal.
He is a successful entrepreneur.

meaning: v. 列举，枚举，一一说明
example: She enumerated the reasons for her decision.
A mile is roughly equivalent to 1.6 kilometers.

meaning: v. 侵蚀，磨损，逐渐破坏
example: Public trust in the government has eroded.

meaning: v. 开发，利用（含负面义）；n. 功绩
example: Companies exploit cheap labor.
The earth's resources are finite.

meaning: v. 制定，构想，系统阐述
example: We need to formulate a clear strategy.

meaning: adj. 脆弱的，易碎的，不稳固的
example: The ceasefire remains fragile.

meaning: adj. 必要的，迫切的；n. 必要之事
example: It is imperative that we act immediately.

meaning: adj. 隐含的，含蓄的，不言而喻的
example: There is an implicit agreement between them.

meaning: adj. 漠不关心的，冷漠的
example: She was indifferent to his suffering.
